rationale: In the citing paper, Zhao, Hoeffler, and Zauberman (2007) is cited repeatedly to justify core hypotheses, especially the claim that simulation works best when it augments a naturally neglected product aspect. That logic is used directly in hypothesis development and in explaining predicted reversals across conditions. The cited paper is also used as methodological precedent for the near-versus-distant time manipulation in Experiment 3. This is therefore a meaningful conceptual and design-level dependency, not a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds directly on Zhao et al. (2007)'s framework about process versus outcome simulation and salience of neglected attributes. It extends that framework by introducing cognitive versus affective processing modes and testing new moderators (product type and temporal frame) in product-evaluation settings.
